STR: +5 HP: +45
WT: 2.5 Size: MEDIUM

Race: ALL

WT: 2.5 Size: MEDIUM

Race: ALL

with so many level restricted items. this is nice. there also all/all +30hp NOT LORE rings that drop in castle mistmore... im seeing a lot of gear id like to have.

#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 14 2001 at 11:37 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) because its betttterr
curious...
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2001 at 1:41 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
why does this cost 6k, when the spider fur cloak, with 1 less strength, 6sv cold, and 4 stamina, which gives around 30 hp perhaps, costs around 2-3k? For 1 strength, 15hp, you have to pay 3k more? Seemed kind of strange to me.
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2003 at 10:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591749845897
236 posts
One might also consider that spider fur cloak drops in Velketor's, which is more of a 40-45-ish zone, whereas this can be gotten in low 30s if you're good. Tougher to get = costlier to buy (sort of). Also, spider fur weighs much less, which adds to value for monks
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Dec 25 2003 at 12:12 AM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
Well there are a lot of things. This is an old world item, and no required or recomended levels. You get those HP at any level. I THINK Stamina works like wis and int? The higher the level the more pool you get from it? Meaning you might not see the full bonus of stamina, where as the HP show up right away. Unfortunately there is a pretty heavy tide of change in MANA and HP items for power leveling new characters. That and super high end stuff. Rarity has a lot to do with it also.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Jan 29 2002 at 2:36 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593361209300
17 posts
Because the pricing data here is reported by players and unreliable at best. Sometimes people put in high prices to increase the precieved value because they have one to sell, but in most cases it is high because it includes dated (i.e., old) data. If you look at the details of the price you will notice the inflation comes from price estimates put in over a year ago. The last 2 prices submitted are 500pp and 750pp which is more in line with current prices following SoV and SoL.
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 9:08 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759939836
121 posts
stamina is different for every class. at level 60, 1 sta = around 1 hp for my druid. so this would give me a whopping 4 or 5 hp.
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 6:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246460789
View User Forum
513 posts
Rarity, my friend... Rarity.
▲ Page top
RE: curious...
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2002 at 5:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761195679
203 posts
Rarity doesn't apply here. The Spiderfur is almost universally better (would say this is better for warriors who solo and use the trick with taking off HP gear to bind wounds higher). No one is going to pay more for an inferior item. The only place rarity really affects prices is on lore items. So the light burlap sack costs more than the evil eye bag, even though the evil eye bag is superior, because people want both.

Of course, rarity can explain why item X, which is only slightly better than item Y, is ten times the value, or why some item that's real special purpose can be really expensive.
▲ Page top
good cloak
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 2:03 AM Rating: Default

this is a great item the hp (45) is good with the 5 str added to it you think more warriors would have it. 10ac is great the next best thing for a warrior is a polar bear cloak and they aint that good for a warrior. this item should be on every mid lvl warriors christmas list.

Actually, AC is nice, but resists are king. Who cares if you have 1100 AC if you get charmed and start beating the hell out of your cleric?

▲ Page top
RE: no
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2001 at 11:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768398088
9 posts
Actually, as a warrior, skip the cloak slot for your resists..CoS is nice , but for a war...naw Lodi Shield or Ancient Seahorse Hide Cloak.
Use Rings and earrings for resists. Any war worth his salt should have resist gear. On a normal day to day routine AC is King..Resists are for the individual circustances that they are needed for. and if you dont know if should have resist gear or ac/hp gear on..your already dead
▲ Page top
Very nice
QuoteReply
# May 16 2001 at 7:46 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592248801112
36 posts
Excellent cloak. Some nice AC (for a cloak) +5 Str (every 5 points in a stat, you hit a milestone where they become more efficient) and +45 HP which at higher levels may seem like nothing (You are hit for 200 damage) but coupled with any other items you have chanced upon, that can be extremely effective. It gives you the 3 most important stats to most melee (if not all). AC, Str, and HP are top priority, bar none. Get this item, now!
▲ Page top
RE: Very nice
QuoteReply
# Aug 16 2001 at 4:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765736404
2 posts
Actually SAVES SAVES SAVES at higher lvls ac don't do jack when you get blasted for 1k+damage a shot. Still is a nice cloak and you can normally get buffs if not you need nice saves(vel rings BD bracers should help out...)

Hello All,

Even with all the new items flooding out of Velius the HBC is still one of the nicer Mid-Price-Range cloaks. 10AC is great for tanks and extra 45 make those Comeplate Heals all the more mana efficant!
